mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which triggers short-term equity price appreciation over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 80 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces central banks to tighten policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 20-day forward returns over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment drop exceeding 50000 signals a sudden labor market contraction that reduces consumer spending capacity and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50000 in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of months following a PAYEMS decline greater than 50000, or the threshold loses predictive accuracy during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate confirms a benign growth environment that supports equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth falls below 2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high inflation indicates stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3 percent annualized, or the signal shows positive predictive power in stagflationary regimes.
